assumptions: Task description was provided and only used as a low-risk planning hint.
Spec text was provided and only used where it did not contradict the contract.
LLM planning note: Baseline cases preserved and enriched with additional functional checks while respecting contract ambiguity.
LLM planning note: No new ports or timing guarantees were invented; all stimuli use only a, b, Cin, y, Co.

expected_properties: Observable outputs respond to legal input changes without assuming internal state.
Checks focus on input/output consistency rather than cycle-accurate timing.
Output y equals a + b + Cin for all driven legal values
Carry-out Co reflects overflow of the 16‑bit addition
cases.goal: Exercise representative legal input combinations and verify functional correctness against a reference model
notes: Case intent is conservative when the contract is weak or timing is unresolved.
Adds reference‑model comparison and random stimulus to broaden basic coverage
preconditions: Use only ports and observable signals present in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Drive a representative legal input pattern across known non-control inputs: ['a', 'b', 'Cin']
Drive a mix of random, sequential, and corner‑case patterns on a, b, and Cin
timing_assumptions: Do not infer internal state or undocumented storage.
Observe outputs after input stabilization; no cycle‑accurate timing assumed

expected_properties: Boundary-value stimulation does not rely on hidden state or undocumented latency.
When width expressions are symbolic, only conservative min/max-style exploration is planned.
Carry propagation chain is correctly handled for max‑value operands
Co is asserted only when the sum exceeds 16 bits
cases.goal: Exercise boundary‑value and width‑sensitive input patterns, especially carry‑chain extremes
notes: Edge coverage remains value-oriented and avoids fixed-latency assumptions.
Adds explicit carry‑propagation scenarios to strengthen edge coverage
preconditions: At least one input or symbolic-width input is available.
stimulus_intent: Use zero-like, one-like, and boundary patterns on ['a', 'b']
Use zero‑like, one‑like, all‑ones, and single‑bit‑carry patterns on a and b, varying Cin to test overflow
timing_assumptions: Do not infer internal state or undocumented storage.
Observe outputs after input stabilization; avoid assumptions about internal latency

expected_properties: Outputs y and Co follow combinational logic for each input change without spurious glitches beyond propagation delay
No hidden state influences successive stimulus evaluations
cases.goal: Validate that rapid successive input changes produce correct combinational results for each stimulus vector
notes: Stimulus sequence alternates between extreme values to stress combinational path
preconditions: All input ports a, b, Cin are driven for each stimulus step
stimulus_intent: Apply a sequence of alternating max (0xFFFF) and min (0x0000) values on a and b, toggling Cin each step
timing_assumptions: Observe outputs after each input vector stabilizes; no clock required

cases.defer_reason: Negative cases require stronger structured illegal-input semantics than the current contract provides.
expected_properties: When inputs contain unknown (X) or high‑impedance (Z) values, the module propagates X/Z to outputs according to Verilog combinational semantics
No illegal internal latch or storage is inferred
cases.goal: Check module robustness to undefined input values
notes: Even though the contract has no illegal input constraints, exercising X/Z helps confirm pure combinational behavior
Negative cases require stronger structured illegal-input semantics than the current contract provides.
preconditions: Drive X or Z on any subset of a, b, Cin
stimulus_intent: Introduce X/Z on a, b, or Cin while keeping other inputs at defined values
timing_assumptions: Observe outputs immediately after input stabilization; no timing guarantees required
